title: [phase:critique] [family:token-implementation] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux wrapper prototype. Review the planned implementation slice: replace --approve with --approval-token carrying signed JSON claims bound to action+target+payload_hash+jti+ttl. Return top 4 implementation ha...
summary_text: 1. **Replay race on `jti` (critical)**: replay protection is check-then-append, not atomic, so concurrent invocations can both pass and execute before either append is visible ([tmux_wrapper.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-wrapper-prototype/tmux_wrapper.py) lines 170-193, 234-237). Missing test: parallel double-submit of same token. 2. **Token consumed before safe preflight (high)**: `ensure_approval()` consumes `jti` before target revalidation and before dry-run branch, so dry-runs and preflight failures burn valid tokens and create false “already used” denials ([tmux_wrapper.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-wrapper-prototype/tmux_wrapper.py) lines 237, 483-487). Missing test: dry-run + failed revalidate should not consume token. 3. **Actor binding is forgeable via environment (high)**: subject check uses `os.environ["USER"]`, which is caller-controlled, not OS identity (`uid/euid`), weakening claim binding ([tmux_wrapper.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-wrapper-prototype/tmux_wrapper.py) line 212). Missing test: spoofed `USER` env should be rejected. 4. **Bearer token exposure via CLI argument (high)**: `--approval-token` in argv can leak through shell history, process inspection, and orchestration logs during TTL window ([tmux_wrapper.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-wrapper-prototype/tmux_wrapper.py) line 408). Missing test/guard: ensure secure input path (stdin/fd/env) and redaction in diagnostics.
